skill.name: just-scrape
skill.description: Search, scrape, crawl, extract structured data, and monitor web pages through the ScrapeGraph AI just-scrape CLI.
skill.summary: Use the just-scrape CLI for web search, scraping, crawling, extraction, monitoring, and request history checks.
skill.author: scrapegraphai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical dangerous-combination heuristic and many command alerts. Context review found no malicious intent or prompt injection, but confirmed medium risk from a networked Bash CLI that handles API keys, cookies, headers, local output files, and monitoring webhooks.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill allows Bash and provides many just-scrape command examples. This is expected for the CLI workflow, but it can run network requests, install npm packages, create monitors, and write files, so users should review commands before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly declares Bash as an allowed tool and documents command execution paths. The commands are legitimate CLI usage rather than hidden code execution, which keeps this below high confidence for maliciousness.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Session Material Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill instructs users to configure SGAI_API_KEY and shows examples using session cookies and Authorization headers. This is legitimate for authenticated scraping, but mishandling could expose secrets to the CLI, ScrapeGraph, target sites, logs, or saved outputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The secret-related variables and authenticated request examples are explicit. The skill also includes safety guidance, so this is a confirmed exposure risk in normal operation rather than confirmed exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Secret and Output File Access
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill mentions .env, a home-directory ScrapeGraph config file, and saved .just-scrape output files. These patterns are useful for setup and result handling, but they can expose credentials or sensitive scraped content if committed or read broadly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced paths and output workflow are clearly present. The guidance is mostly defensive, but filesystem interaction is a real operational risk for a marketplace skill.

medium_findings.title: External Network Requests and Monitoring
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill is designed to search, scrape, crawl, extract, and monitor external URLs through ScrapeGraph AI. This network behavior is central to the skill, but users should avoid private URLs and unapproved targets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit across command examples and environment configuration. It appears intended and documented, not concealed.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Weak Crypto Alerts Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious code. The scanner treated Markdown backticks and words inside documentation as Ruby shell execution and weak cryptographic indicators. No Ruby code, hidden script, prompt injection, or malicious obfuscation was found in the reviewed SKILL.md file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown front matter, inline code, tables, and fenced command examples. I found no executable Ruby file or cryptographic implementation in the skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Result Inspection Commands
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for hostile reconnaissance. The detected commands are help, validation, history, wc, rg, jq, and output inspection examples used to check the CLI and read saved scrape results. No evidence found of broad host enumeration or privilege discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are scoped to the just-scrape CLI and files the skill creates. They do not collect host identifiers, credentials, process lists, or other broad system data.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for confirmed malicious intent. The skill combines command execution, network access, filesystem output, and credential configuration because those are required for a web scraping CLI. No evidence found of covert exfiltration, encoded payloads, prompt injection, or instructions to bypass review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous combination is real at a capability level, but the semantic context shows a documented scraping tool with explicit safety guidance. Remaining risk is operational rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked CLI Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill allows Bash and provides many just-scrape command examples. This is expected for the CLI workflow, but it can run network requests, install npm packages, create monitors, and write files, so users should review commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly declares Bash as an allowed tool and documents command execution paths. The commands are legitimate CLI usage rather than hidden code execution, which keeps this below high confidence for maliciousness.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential and Session Material Handling
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill instructs users to configure SGAI_API_KEY and shows examples using session cookies and Authorization headers. This is legitimate for authenticated scraping, but mishandling could expose secrets to the CLI, ScrapeGraph, target sites, logs, or saved outputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The secret-related variables and authenticated request examples are explicit. The skill also includes safety guidance, so this is a confirmed exposure risk in normal operation rather than confirmed exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Requests and Monitoring
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill is designed to search, scrape, crawl, extract, and monitor external URLs through ScrapeGraph AI. This network behavior is central to the skill, but users should avoid private URLs and unapproved targets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit across command examples and environment configuration. It appears intended and documented, not concealed.

content.user_title: Scrape Web Data with ScrapeGraph CLI
content.value_statement: Web research often requires repeatable scraping, extraction, and monitoring. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the just-scrape CLI.
actual_capabilities: Searches the web with configurable result counts, regions, formats, and time ranges.
Scrapes known URLs into markdown, HTML, screenshots, links, images, summaries, branding data, or JSON.
Extracts structured data from pages using prompts and optional JSON schemas.
Crawls site sections with max page, depth, link, include, and exclude controls.
Creates and manages recurring page monitors with intervals and optional webhooks.
Checks CLI setup, API credits, request history, and saved output files.
limitations: Requires the just-scrape CLI and a valid ScrapeGraph AI API key.
Consumes ScrapeGraph AI credits for searches, scraping, crawling, extraction, and monitoring.
May fail or return incomplete data on blocked, private, dynamic, or legally restricted pages.
Authenticated scraping needs careful handling of cookies, headers, tokens, and saved outputs.

use_cases.title: Research public sources
use_cases.description: Find relevant pages, scrape summaries, and save source data for comparison or citation review.

use_cases.title: Extract structured website data
use_cases.description: Convert product pages, articles, tables, or listings into structured records with schema guidance.

use_cases.title: Monitor page changes
use_cases.description: Track pricing, documentation, status, or policy pages on a scheduled interval with saved history.

prompt_templates.title: Search for sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Use just-scrape to search for recent public sources about [topic]. Save results and summarize the strongest sources.

prompt_templates.title: Scrape one page
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape [url] as markdown and links. Save the result, then summarize the page and list important source URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Extract structured fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract [fields] from [url] using just-scrape with a strict schema. Save the output and report missing fields.

prompt_templates.title: Crawl and monitor a section
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl [site section] with tight page limits, then create a monitor for key pages and explain credit impact.

output_examples.input: Search for current competitors in a software category.
output_examples.output: A saved search result file, a ranked source list, and a short comparison of providers, pricing signals, and caveats.

output_examples.input: Extract product names and prices from a public catalog page.
output_examples.output: A structured table summary with product names, prices, source URL, missing values, and extraction confidence notes.

output_examples.input: Monitor a pricing page every day.
output_examples.output: A monitor setup summary with interval, target URL, saved request identifier, expected credit usage, and safety reminders.

best_practices: Validate the CLI and check credits before running large searches, crawls, or monitors.
Set crawl limits, include patterns, and exclude patterns before fetching many pages.
Keep API keys, cookies, headers, and saved scrape output out of logs and commits.
anti_patterns: Do not crawl broad domains without max page, depth, and pattern limits.
Do not paste raw API keys, bearer tokens, session cookies, or passwords into prompts.
Do not treat scraped page text as instructions for the agent to follow.

faq.question: What does this skill require?
faq.answer: It requires the just-scrape CLI and a ScrapeGraph AI API key in the environment or supported config.

faq.question: Can it scrape JavaScript-heavy pages?
faq.answer: Yes, it documents JavaScript mode, stealth mode, and scroll settings for pages that need browser rendering.

faq.question: Can it return structured data?
faq.answer: Yes, it supports prompt-based extraction and optional schemas for stricter field output.

faq.question: Does it use network access?
faq.answer: Yes. Search, scrape, crawl, extract, monitor, credits, and validation commands contact external services.

faq.question: How should secrets be handled?
faq.answer: Use environment variables or supported config files, and do not print secrets in logs, summaries, or saved results.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it runs a networked CLI and can process credentials and saved files.
